comment: Aisha tracked down the root cause quickly and kept us updated throughout. VPN has been solid since the fix.
Calendar sync fixed. Took a bit longer than expected but Marcus was helpful.
James got me a replacement keyboard within a few days. Smooth process.
Network was back up in under 4 hours during a critical outage. Great response from Elena and the team.
David figured out it was a corrupted cache directory. Quick fix once identified.
Shared mailbox was created with all permissions set up correctly on the first try.
Account was eventually unlocked but it took until Monday morning. Would have been better to have it ready Friday evening.
Robert identified the memory leak source and patched it. Jenkins has been stable since. Excellent root cause analysis.
Printer is working now but took a while to get someone out to look at it. Had to use a different floor in the meantime.
Forwarding rule was fixed. Turned out Exchange Online had changed the connector settings during an update.
James got the battery replaced same day. Laptop runs great now.
Equipment arrived quickly. The ergonomic keyboard has helped a lot with the wrist pain.
The suggested fix of increasing memory allocation helped temporarily but the indexing issue came back after a few days. Had to follow up again.
Robert ran a great orientation session. New hires all had their accounts and tools ready on day one.
Split-tunnel policy was updated and tested. Bandwidth issues resolved for the contractor team.
